title: Concentration inequalities
summary: Tail and moment bounds controlling how sums and functions of independent (or martingale) random variables deviate from their means.

theorems.name: Hoeffding's inequality (sub-Gaussian sum)
theorems.blurb: For independent zero-mean sub-Gaussian variables, the probability the sum exceeds t decays like a Gaussian tail in t.

theorems.name: Cramér–Chernoff bound
theorems.blurb: The foundational moment-generating-function tail bound obtained by combining monotonicity of the exponential with Markov's inequality.

theorems.name: Bernstein's inequality (sub-exponential sum)
theorems.blurb: For independent zero-mean sub-exponential variables, the tail interpolates between Gaussian and exponential decay depending on the deviation scale.

theorems.name: Azuma–Hoeffding inequality
theorems.blurb: A bounded martingale-difference sequence concentrates with a Gaussian-type tail, giving a martingale analogue of Hoeffding's inequality.

theorems.name: McDiarmid's bounded-differences inequality
theorems.blurb: A function of independent variables that changes by at most c_i when one coordinate changes concentrates sharply around its mean.

theorems.name: Efron–Stein inequality
theorems.blurb: Bounds the variance of a function of independent variables by the expected squared effect of resampling each coordinate independently.
